reforms.description: The Danish government aims to raise research and development (R&D) investment to enhance productivity growth, focusing on improving framework conditions and promoting competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D investment is expected to lead to higher productivity and innovation, benefiting the overall economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has stated its intention to raise R&D investment as part of its strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy direction aimed at enhancing R&D investment, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Raising research and development (R&D) investment, currently close to the average EU level, could contribute to higher total factor productivity growth.
